mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of high inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5d returns in > 60% of high-inflation periods, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month federal funds rate hike exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances following a >0.25% rate hike, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales decline while GDP remains positive, indicates weakening consumer demand that precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 5d returns with a win rate above 50% over a 3-year period, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, simultaneous increases in industrial production and decreases in unemployment signal accelerating economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined INDPRO-UNRATE signal yields negative 20d returns in > 60% of post-recovery periods, or predictive accuracy falls below random chance over a 5-year window.
